message:
feat(messaging,bin): root wildcard support, fabric re-exports, non-quiescent close calibration scenario

src/messaging/subject.rs:
- Fix root wildcard patterns: bare '>' now matches all subjects (global
  tail wildcard), bare '*' matches single-token subjects, and
  SubjectLookup correctly routes root wildcards through the trie
- Add NamespaceKernelAnalysis with analyze_kernel_coverage() for namespace
  partitioning statistics (total subjects, covered, uncovered, overlap)
- Add comprehensive tests: root wildcards edge cases, kernel analysis
  coverage, namespace overlap detection, empty/degenerate patterns

src/messaging/mod.rs:
- Add pub use re-exports for core Fabric types (CapturePolicy, Fabric,
  FabricMessage, FabricReply, FabricStreamConfig, FabricStreamHandle,
  FabricSubscription, PublishReceipt) gated on messaging-fabric feature

src/bin/asupersync.rs:
- Add calibration.region.close.non_quiescent differential scenario that
  intentionally closes a region with live children to prove the
  quiescence violation is detected and escalated as a RuntimeSemanticBug
- Add run_calibration_region_non_quiescent_close_scenario() implementation
  with dual lab/live capture, obligation balance, and artifact bundle
- Register scenario in is_calibration_scenario() match arm

tests/lab_live_verification_taxonomy_contract.rs:
- Add calibration.region.close.non_quiescent to calibration scenario list
  and surface-to-inventory assertions
- Update gap statement from 'T4 anchor still missing' to reference the
  new calibration scenario

tests/fabric_stream_lab_integration.rs:
- Update imports to use re-exported Fabric types from messaging::

tests/tokio_compatibility_crate_scaffolding_policy.rs:
- Bump compatibility_line from 0.1.x to 0.2.x

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
